Member-only story

Spec-Driven Development has three levels: spec-first, spec-anchored, and spec-as-source. Learn to name your team’s level and calculate the cost of moving up one rung.

11 min read
Just now

--

“The beginning of wisdom is to call things by their proper names.” — Confucius, Philosopher (traditionally attributed to Analects XIII.3)
Everyone says they “do Spec-Driven Development” — but a single name conceals three completely different practices that produce completely different results. The same label gets praised and mocked simultaneously, because three teams saying “we do SDD” are doing three different things and comparing the incomparable. This isn’t an academic dispute.
An analysis of three real tools revealed something uncomfortable: most teams are stuck on the lowest level and are quietly paying the cost of the highest one — carrying overhead they didn’t anticipate, without the benefit they expected. Over the next nine minutes, you’ll learn to name the level your team is actually working at, calculate the cost of moving up one rung, and decide whether it’s worth it. Because it’s the choice of rung — not simply “doing SDD” — that determines the outcome.
